name: Persian Gulf
climate: Hyper-arid desert; extremely hot summers exceeding 50C; mild winters; minimal rainfall; high humidity along coastal areas

neighbors.name: Strait of Hormuz
neighbors.relevance: Sole maritime exit from the Gulf; all seaborne oil exports must transit this chokepoint

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: Six of eight Gulf littoral states form the GCC economic bloc

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Connected via Saudi Arabia's dual coastline; alternative energy export corridor

neighbors.name: Levant
neighbors.relevance: Northern neighbor via Iraq; pipeline routes and geopolitical spillover

neighbors.name: OPEC
neighbors.relevance: Gulf states dominate OPEC production and spare capacity

links.description: All Gulf seaborne crude and LNG exports funnel through the Strait of Hormuz
GCC members comprise the Arab side of the Persian Gulf littoral
SAU, IRN, IRQ, KWT, ARE are OPEC members accounting for most Gulf production
East Asia is the dominant buyer of Gulf crude oil and LNG
China is the largest single buyer of Gulf oil; BRI infrastructure investments across the region
Saudi East-West pipeline and Red Sea ports provide partial bypass of Hormuz
Gulf oil and gas bound for Europe transits Red Sea and Suez Canal
